type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Wood waste incinerator was shut down in 2002 and did not operate in 2003, this results in a reduction of NOX emissions. Other source for this is emissions from natural gas fired kiln and building furnaces. This was as reduced by just over 10% as a result

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Lumber drying kilns fired by natural gas and building heaters constitute the emission of carbon monoxide. Dry and warm weather conditions combined with a reduction in production reduced overall drying requirements.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Increases in disposal volumes from 2002, 675kgs to 1500kgs in 2003 are a result of construction waste during a plant conversion to arsenic free products.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Releases in 2003 are a result of rainwater washing small amounts off of product stored in the yard. A very dry year reduced the amount of potential leaching off of product. Production of product was also down as a result of market conditions.

Also clea

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Additional modifications to the plant required the removal and disposal of construction material including gravel and concrete with surface staining which included chromium.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Reduction in use of the chromium based wood preservatives will reduce the potential for on-site releases. Conversion to these new products in 2004.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Additional site clean-up and waste disposal as a result was higher than 2002. Some concrete which is very heavy was also dispossed of during a plant conversion to new wood preservatives.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Reduction in copper values for 2003 from 2002 values are a result of clean-up efforts in 2002 which addressed most copper related clean-up from a release in early 2002.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Increase in volume of soil transfered to an off-site location for disposal increase from around 203kgs in 2001 to 675kgs in 2002 as a result of remediation efforts due to a release from the plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Identified a release in April of 2002. Reported to provincial authorities and remediation measures were commenced. Unknown amount of Ar was released, could be as high as 10kgs as reported in the above information field.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Increased Chromium disposal as a result of remediation efforts related to a release and clean-up efforts.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: As noted, clean up from a release increase amount transfered to an off-site facility.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
A release from the plant was identified in April 2002. Estimates of amount released are difficult due to location of release. Estimated at 10kgs of liquid coper. 1kg Remedial measures and release reporting to provincial authorities was compliant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
All water disposed of via sanitary sewer line to the Town of Cochrane meets the City of Calgary/Town of Cochrane sanitary discharge criteria. The Town of Cochrane handles the treatment and recycling of the water.

Based on total production:
Reporting Threshold is 50kg (manufactured, processed or otherwise used)					
Threshold Met:   Yes -	5.91	MT

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Water from our retention pond is disposed of to the Town Of Cochrane sanitary line. The water prior to disposal is tested and meets City of Calgary/Town of Cochrane sanitary discharge criteria.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
All water disposed of via sanitary sewer line to the Town of Cochrane meets the City of Calgary/Town of Cochrane sanitary discharge criteria. The Town of Cochrane handles the treatment and recycling of the water.

If chromium accounts for 52% of the mass of CrO3 then the mass of Cr is (kg)						6608.238   or in (MT)	6.61.

comments.comment: Previously the PM emissions did not include road dust calculations. 2021 NPRI will be updated to include the road dust calculations for all PMs calculations.

comments.comment: In the previous years road dust calculations for PMs were not reported. the 2021 will be updated to include road dust for all PM emissions.
